games.title: Cuddle Buddies
games.rules: Take turns drawing a card from any of the three decks. Use the prompt as inspiration for a cuddly moment. Consent is important. Only proceed with an action if everyone agrees to do so.

decks.label: The Friendzone
prompts: Sit back-to-back and relax together for 1 minute
Link arms while walking across the room
Rest your head briefly on their shoulder
Give each other a high-five that turns into holding hands for 5 seconds
Sit side-by-side with your legs touching
Lean your knees against each other while sitting
Give a 10-second one-armed side hug
Stand shoulder-to-shoulder and sway gently for 20 seconds
Gently tap or trace shapes on their hand — they guess what it is
Sit cross-legged facing each other, knees touching
Share a blanket over both your laps
Rest your hand lightly on their forearm during a conversation
Mirror each other’s posture and maintain eye contact for 30 seconds
Give a slow, deliberate handshake that lingers
Sit close enough that your hips touch and stay there for a minute
Place your hand on top of theirs and breathe together for 5 breaths
Lightly bump shoulders and stay leaned in
Let your feet touch under the table for 1 minute
Gently guide them by the elbow across the room
Sit side-by-side and gently bump hips every few seconds for 1 minute
Share a brief two-handed handshake and hold for 10 seconds
Sit facing each other and lightly press your palms together
Walk across the room with your shoulders touching the whole way
Tap a rhythm on their arm and have them copy it
Draw a sketch on their back and have them guess what it is
Sit close and lightly rest your elbow against theirs
Stand next to each other and gently lean heads together for 10 seconds
Sit with your backs touching and take 5 deep breaths together
Hook pinkies together and hold for 20 seconds
Rest your foot lightly on top of theirs
Gently nudge them with your knee and keep contact for 30 seconds
Sit close and read something silently side-by-side while touching
Lightly tap their shoulder and leave your hand there for 5 seconds
Stand in front of each other and do a synchronized stretch
Sit close and pass an object hand-to-hand slowly, brushing fingers
Gently guide their hand to mirror a simple motion (like drawing a circle)
Sit hip-to-hip and sway slightly together
Blindfold them, then take them by the arm and walk through the room
Do a “trust lean” where you gently lean into each other while standing

decks.label: Cuddle Buds
prompts: Sit with one person leaning fully into the other’s chest
Side hug that turns into a full 20-second hug
Sit between their legs while both are upright
Rest your head in their lap for 1 minute
Wrap up together in a blanket cocoon
Sit facing each other with legs intertwined
One person gently strokes the other’s arm for 30 seconds
Lay side-by-side with your heads touching
Give each other a slow, full hug without talking
Sit on the couch with one person’s legs draped over the other
Lean your head against theirs and stay still
Sit behind them and gently hold their shoulders
“Half spoon” while sitting (one person slightly behind, arms around)
Hold each other in a quiet embrace for 1 minute
One person plays with the other’s hair
Lay with your legs tangled together
Sit chest-to-chest and match breathing
One person rests their hand on the other’s back
Sit side-by-side with one person’s head resting fully on the other’s shoulder
One person wraps both arms around the other from behind while seated
Lay with your heads close and gently bump noses playfully
Sit together and trace circles on each other’s hands
Rest your foreheads together while holding hands
Sit close and gently squeeze each other in a soft hug for 30 seconds
Lay side-by-side and lightly hold each other’s wrists
Sit behind them and rest your chin on their shoulder
Sit facing each other and hold both hands while leaning in close
One person drapes an arm across the other’s chest while sitting together
Sit with your legs overlapping and gently shift closer
Lay together with one arm wrapped around each other’s waist
Sit close and lightly brush your cheek against theirs
One person cups the other’s hands between both of theirs
Sit with your heads touching and gently sway side-to-side
Lay together and trace shapes on each other’s back
Sit in a loose cuddle and gently rock side to side
Rest your head on their chest and listen to their breathing
Sit close and intertwine fingers while leaning into each other
Lay with one person partially curled into the other
Sit on the floor with one person leaning fully into the other’s torso
Try a full spooning position for 1–2 minutes

decks.label: Straightup Foreplay
prompts: Sit straddling their lap (clothed) and maintain eye contact
Slow, close full-body hug with hips aligned
Sit between their legs and gently lean back into them
Rock gently together while sitting close
Lay partially on top of each other (torso-to-torso)
Wrap legs around each other while sitting
Sit facing each other, legs intertwined, bodies close
Slow grinding motion while both fully clothed (keep it subtle and playful)
Lay with one leg draped fully over their hips
Sit on their lap and lean chest-to-chest
Full-body spooning with tighter contact
Press foreheads together while bodies stay fully connected
Gentle hip-to-hip movement while hugging
Lay between their legs while facing them
Sit straddling one thigh and lean forward
Hold each other tightly while slowly shifting weight together
Sit close enough that your entire bodies are touching and move together slightly
One person pulls the other closer by the waist and holds
Slow, playful “almost grinding” with exaggerated pauses
Sit very close and slowly pull each other in by the waist
Lay chest-to-chest and gently shift your weight together
Sit straddling their lap and slowly lean in, stopping just short of contact
Wrap your arms around each other and slowly tighten the embrace
Sit between their legs and gently press your back into them
Lay side-by-side and slowly slide one leg between theirs
Sit close and gently guide each other’s hips into small movements
Press your bodies together and hold still while maintaining eye contact
Sit on their lap and slowly rock side-to-side
Lay with your torso pressed to theirs and lightly trace along their sides
Sit close and alternate gently pulling each other closer
Wrap legs together and slowly adjust to increase contact
Sit chest-to-chest and slowly lean forward and back together
One person holds the other firmly at the waist while staying close
Sit straddling one leg and slowly shift your weight
Lay together and gently press hips closer, then relax, repeating slowly
Sit close and move in sync as if slow dancing while seated
Hold a tight embrace and slowly sway your bodies together
Sit face-to-face and slowly close the distance between your bodies
Maintain full-body contact and experiment with slow, synchronized movement
Stay in a close straddle position and synchronize breathing and movement

games.title: WNRS
games.rules: Each player draws one card per round, choosing freely from any of the three decks. Reflect quietly for a moment before answering — depth is rewarded over speed. Others may ask one follow-up question after each answer. When a Wildcard is drawn, the question is posed to the entire group simultaneously. Each person writes their answer before anyone speaks — then all answers are revealed at once.

prompts.text: What was your first impression of me?
What do you think is the hardest part of what I do for a living
What reality show do you think I'm most likely to binge watch? explain.
Do I seem like a cat or dog person?
What do my shoes tell you about me?
Do I seem like more of a creative or analytical type? explain.

Do I look kind? explain.
Do you think I fall in love easily? why or why not?
As a child, what do you think I wanted to be?

Close your eyes. What color is my shirt?
Ask and answer the next question in a different accent. (Both players)
Draw a picture together. (30 seconds)
Write down something others would never guess about you just by looking at you. (Both players) Compare.
Draw a portrait of each other to the best of your ability. (1 minute) Then exchange.
Think of your favorite brand of cereal. On the count of three say your answers out loud! (Both players)
Think of your favorite childhood tv show of all time. On the count of three, say it out loud! (Both players)
Rate your dancing skills on a scale of 1-10. On the count of three, say your answers out loud! (Both players)
Close your eyes. What color are my eyes?
Make an assumption about me. (Both players)

Staring contest. First to blink must reveal a personal problem and ask your partner for advice on how they might handle it.

games.title: WNRS Family
games.rules: Warning: Not your average game night. Starting with level 1, players take turns asking and answering questions from each deck. Each player must ask and answer at least 3 cards before progressing to the next level. The person who did the dishes last draws the first card. Level 1: On your turn, draw a card and have the player to your left answer. (Option for all players to chime in.) Continue in a circle until Level 1 is complete. Level 2: When it's your turn, read your card out loud and choose one player in the group to answer. You may not select a player who has just been picked. Level 3: When it's your turn, draw a card and the group collectively answers for the cardholder. Wildcards: If you draw a wildcard, your group must complete the instructions unless otherwise stated.

prompts.text: What do you think my favorite/least favorite holiday is? Explain.
From your perspective, how would you describe my relationship with work or school?
How important do you think birthdays are to me? Explain.
For siblings: Growing up, who got away with more?
If our family had a Netflix series, what would it be called?
If I have the aux on a family road trip, what am I most likely to put on?
Who is most likely to read through the terms and conditions?
For children to ask parents: In what ways was my childhood different than yours? Get specific.
Who in our family do you feel you know the most/least? Why do you think that is?
What warning sign should be outside our family home?
Describe what you think a day in my life looks like. Get specific.
How do I show you I'm in a bad mood without telling you?
Who would you cast to play each of us in a movie based on our life?
In one word, how would you describe my role in the family?
If we were on Family Feud, who would win the most points for us?
Growing up, how strict do you feel our household was on a scale of 1-10? Explain.
What would you bring home to cheer me up after I've had a bad day?
Do you think I ever snuck out of the house growing up? Why or why not?
What is my comfort TV show and how many times do you think I've watched it?
What do you think was my strongest subject in school? My weakest?
What compliment do you think I hear the most? What don't I hear enough?
What am I really good at that perhaps I'm unaware of?
Do you think I've found my passion in life? What do you think it is?
Who do you think I get my sense of humor from?
Who in our family do you think I'm most similar to? Who am I the most different from? Explain.
Which one of us is most likely to lead the group out of an escape room successfully?
If our family made a cookbook, what meals would be included?
If our family came out with a line of merch, what phrase would we print on a t-shirt?
What am I most likely to get fired for?
Which friend of mine do you think brings out the best in me? What do they bring out?
What consistently brings a smile to my face?
If I were a cartoon character, what would my signature outfit be?
What do you think I am/was like in school?
Who in our family would you most likely go to for financial advice? What would you ask?
Who in the family are you most likely to go to for relationship advice? What would you ask them?
What do you think I wanted to be when I grew up?
Who in our family is most likely to get Tiktok famous? What would they go viral for?
How messy do you think my room is right now on a scale of 1-10? Explain.
What do you think is taking up most of my energy these days? How do you think I feel about it?
How would you describe my relationship with social media in one word?
Who do you think my best friend is and why?
All players rate how unfiltered you feel you can be with our family on a scale of 1-10. Compare and explain.
Who is most likely to be late to a family gathering? All players say your answers out loud on the count of 3.
All players set your phone to do not disturb. Which player is this going to be the hardest for?
Which of us is most likely to drive somewhere every day and still get lost?
Tell the family member to your right one thing you admire about them and why.
Each player writes down one thing that helps you open up emotionally and one thing that shuts you down. Share your answers with the family before moving on to the next card.
In one sentence, describe the home you grew up in. 1 minute. Share.
Collectively rate your family members from best to worst texter. Write it down. Compare and explain.
What do you think our family's superpower is? Write it down. Compare and explain.
Give each family member a superlative (Best dressed, most likely to befriend a stranger, most likely to cry in public, etc). Get creative.
Write down how open-minded you think our family is on a scale of 1-10. 10 being extremely open-minded. Compare and explain.
Tell the group your favorite memory that you share with the person sitting on your left.
Who in the family has gotten the most speeding tickets? Everyone says your answer out loud on the count of 3.
Take a family selfie! Bonus: Send it to someone who wishes were here.
Which one of us is most likely to put an ice cream carton back in the fridge with just one bite left? Everyone says your answer out loud on the count of 3.
Reminder There's always another layer to everyone you think you know best.
What's your favorite thing about being home?
How likely are you to bring a significant other home to meet us? What is something you'd warn them about beforehand?
Who do you consider family that you're not related to? What is their name and one thing about them?
What is the most valuable lesson our family has taught you about work ethic? Explain.
What is the most valuable lesson our family has taught you about love? Explain.
What boundary do you wish you had set with our family sooner? How can we honor it now?
What conversation feels overdue in our family? Go easy or go deep.
What's your favorite thing about this chapter in your life?
How does our family deal with conflict? What's one small thing we can work on?
Is there anything you've struggled to share with our family? Permission to share now if you feel ready.
What are you proudest of yourself for getting through this past year?
What are you proudest of our family for getting through?
What area of your life have you gained more confidence in recently? What's helped you get there?
When do you come to our family for help instead of anyone else?
What is a family memory that still makes you laugh to this day?
What's your grandfather's name and one thing about him?
When was the last time you took a risk that paid off?
What have you learned to accept about yourself over time? What's helped you get there?

What's your most vivid memory from your childhood home? Describe in detail.
How do you show up for yourself after a bad day?
What is the best gift a family member has ever given you? Material or Immaterial?
When was the last time you felt most understood by someone in the family? What did they understand?
When was the last time you felt misunderstood by someone in the family? What do you wish they understood?
When was the last time you took someone in our family for granted?

What's your grandmother's name and one thing about her?
What's been the best mistake you've ever made?
Where do our family arguments often stem from? How do you personally contribute?
What's become more/less important to you over the years?
For siblings: What family rules have you learned to appreciate as you've gotten older?
How do you define family beyond just being related? Get specific.
For siblings: What's a vivid memory that you have of me growing up that makes you smile to this day?
What are you currently working through that our family doesn't see?
Finish the sentence: Something that brought a smile to my face this week was _________.
For parents to answer: How would you describe raising this family in one word?
What's one lesson you've had to unlearn from growing up in our family? Big or small.
What's something that felt normal growing up that you now realize is special?
What's something you want the next generation of our family to let go of?
Describe your perfect day with the family. Get specific.
All players rate our family's communication skills on a scale of 1-10. How could we improve that by 1 point?
Apologize to someone in the group for something big or small.
All players write down your favorite family memory that comes to mind. 1 minute. Compare.
Cardholder, share a life update with the family you would usually just keep to yourself.
Play a round of rock paper scissors with the person sitting in front of you. Loser must complete a house chore of the winner's choice.
Initiate a family group hug! Not the crappy kind. A warm fluffy one!
Cardholder, pick a family member who isn't playing the game to text "I love you" to, before flipping to the next card.
Reminder Everyone is doing the best they can with what they know.
How would you describe our family to a stranger?

What is something about us that only our family would understand?
For children to answer: Finish the sentence, I'm thankful you raised me to be _________.
What do you think I should let go of?
Finish this sentence: Before this game is over I want our family to know _________.

Who deserves a round of applause? What for?
What do you need today?
How do our personalities complement each other?
From your perspective, what is something I do that seems effortless?
When do you miss our family the most?
What did you learn about yourself throughout this game?
What surprised you most during the game? Or who?
Who did you learn the most about during the game? What did you learn?
Who is the best communicator in our family? What can we learn from them?
What do you think I would benefit from doing less of?
What do you think I would benefit from doing more of?
What do you think our family would benefit from doing more of?
What subject is our family most qualified to give advice about?
What's something I could be great at if I invested the time?
Finish the sentence: One small way I can better show up for myself is _________.
Finish the sentence: One small way I can better show up for our family is _________.
For parents to answer: In what ways do you look up to your children?
What contribution do I bring to our family that I don't get enough credit for?
What does our family deserve an award for?
What do you need from our family in this chapter of your life?
What am I most qualified to give advice about?
Tell the family member to your right one reason you're proud of them and why.
Tell the person across from you the biggest lesson you've learned from them. Explain.
Is there anything you've always wanted to ask a family member but haven't? Permission to finally ask.
Cardholder, make a toast to the family.
Each player writes out one thing our family would benefit from letting go of. Share and tear!
Thank someone in the group for something big or small.
Plan a family gathering for the near future. Add to your calendars and follow through.
All players draw a family portrait to the best of your ability. 1 minute. Vote for which one goes on the fridge.
Create a family theme song. Each player writes one line and sing together once complete. Get into it.
Reminder Boundaries allow us to love at full capacity.
Reminder Everyone's love looks different.

games.title: WNRS Couples
games.rules: Warning: Not your average game night. Starting with level 1, players take turns asking and answering questions from each deck. Each player must ask and answer at least 3 cards before progressing to the next level. The person who did the dishes last draws the first card. Level 1: On your turn, draw a card and have the player to your left answer. (Option for all players to chime in.) Continue in a circle until Level 1 is complete. Level 2: When it's your turn, read your card out loud and choose one player in the group to answer. You may not select a player who has just been picked. Level 3: When it's your turn, draw a card and the group collectively answers for the cardholder. Wildcards: If you draw a wildcard, your group must complete the instructions unless otherwise stated.

games.title: Gestern Heute Morgen
games.rules: Each player draws one card per round, choosing freely from any of the three decks. Reflect quietly for a moment before answering — depth is rewarded over speed. Others may ask one follow-up question after each answer. When a Wildcard is drawn, the question is posed to the entire group simultaneously. Each person writes their answer before anyone speaks — then all answers are revealed at once.
